{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 子安地蔵寺 |画像 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷94 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 易産山 |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 = [[地蔵菩薩]] |創建年 = [[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） |開基 = [[行基菩薩]] |正式名 = 易産山 地蔵寺 |別称 = 子安地蔵寺、ふじの寺 |札所等 = [[関西花の寺二十五霊場]] 第24番
紀伊之国十三仏霊場 第5番
紀伊西国霊場 第2番 |公式HP = http://www8.ocn.ne.jp/~jizouji/ |公式HP名 = ふじの寺 子安地蔵寺 }} '''地蔵寺'''(通称子安地蔵寺)は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 本寺は天平九年（737年）の春、[[行基菩薩]]の開基にて本尊地蔵菩薩も行基菩薩の御手刻の御尊像とされており女人安産守護として安置されたもの。 [[天正]]九年([[1581]]年)信長の勢、信孝による[[高野攻め]]の時兵火にあい、地蔵堂は焼失したが、御本尊は人里離れた山中に難をのがれたのを村人によって発見された。 その後、[[紀伊]]大納言頼宣公によって、お堂を建立なされ地蔵尊を安置し、[[正保]]五年（[[1648年]]）田園２町歩を本尊香花の費に供養され、[[南龍院]]は11回に渡って参詣された。

{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 子安地蔵寺 |画像 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷94 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |山号 = 易産山 |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 = [[地蔵菩薩]] |創建年 = [[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） |開基 = [[行基菩薩]] |正式名 = 易産山 地蔵寺 |別称 = 子安地蔵寺、ふじの寺 |札所等 = [[関西花の寺二十五霊場]] 第24番
紀伊之国十三仏霊場 第5番
紀伊西国霊場 第2番 |公式HP = http://www8.ocn.ne.jp/~jizouji/ |公式HP名 = ふじの寺 子安地蔵寺 }} '''地蔵寺'''(通称子安地蔵寺)は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。 本寺は天平九年（737年）の春、[[行基菩薩]]の開基にて本尊地蔵菩薩も行基菩薩の御手刻の御尊像とされており女人安産守護として安置されたもの。 [[天正]]九年([[1581年]])[[織田信長]]の勢、[[織田信孝]]による高野攻め（[[紀州征伐]]）の時兵火にあい、地蔵堂は焼失したが、御本尊は人里離れた山中に難をのがれたのを村人によって発見された。 その後、[[紀伊大納言]][[徳川頼宣]]によって、お堂を建立し地蔵尊を安置し、[[正保]]五年（[[1648年]]）田園２町歩を本尊香花の費に供養され、頼宣(南龍院)は11回に渡って参詣された。

紀伊西国霊場 第2番 |公式HP = http://www8.ocn.ne.jp/~jizouji/ |公式HP名 = ふじの寺 子安地蔵寺 }} '''地蔵寺'''（じぞうじ、通称：'''子安地蔵寺'''・'''藤の寺'''）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[仏教]][[寺院]]。 本寺は天平九年（737年）の春、[[行基菩薩]]の開基にて本尊地蔵菩薩も行基菩薩の御手刻の御尊像とされており女人安産守護として安置されたもの。 [[天正]]九年（[[1581年]]）[[織田信長]]の勢、[[織田信孝]]による高野攻め（[[紀州征伐]]）の時兵火にあい、地蔵堂は焼失したが、御本尊は人里離れた山中に難をのがれたのを村人によって発見された。 その後、[[紀伊大納言]][[徳川頼宣]]によって、お堂を建立し地蔵尊を安置し、[[正保]]五年（[[1648年]]）田園２町歩を本尊香花の費に供養され、頼宣（南龍院）は11回に渡って参詣された。 {{デフォルトソート:こやすしそうし}} [[Category:高野山真言宗]] [[Category:和歌山県の寺]] [[Category:橋本市]]

{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD易產山護國院地藏寺|易產山護國院地藏寺（受付依頼）]]、[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD易産山護国院地蔵寺|易産山護国院地蔵寺（受付依頼）]]|※編集上のご注意！このテンプレートはBotによって自動貼付、および審議終了後およそ1週間で自動除去されます。審議途中でリダイレクト先を変更した場合や記事に置き換えた場合、またリダイレクトが削除された後でもテンプレートを取り除かないでください。前後に使用されているnoinclude文も同様です。手動で取り除いた場合、再度自動貼付されることがあります。}} __NOTOC__ {{出典の明記|date=2016年8月21日 (日) 23:41 (UTC)}} {{日本の寺院 |名称 = 子安地蔵寺 |画像 = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
本堂（左奥）と水子地蔵尊（手前右） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷94 |位置 = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |山号 = 易産山（易產山） |院号 = 護國院 |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 = [[地蔵菩薩]] |創建年 = [[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） |開基 = [[行基]] |正式名 = 易産山護国院地蔵寺
（易產山護國院地藏寺） |別称 = 子安地蔵寺 |札所等 = [[関西花の寺二十五霊場]]第24番
[[紀伊之国十三仏霊場]]第5番
[[紀伊西国霊場]]第2番 |公式HP = http://hananotera.com |公式HP名 = 子安地蔵寺 公式ウェブサイト |地図=Japan Wakayama }} '''子安地蔵寺'''（こやすじぞうじ）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷にある[[高野山真言宗]]の寺院。山号は易産山（いさんざん）。「[[ふじ]]{{要曖昧さ回避|date=2021年5月}}の寺」として知られる。 == 歴史 == 伝承によれば、天平9年（737年）の春、[[行基]]の開基で、本尊の[[地蔵菩薩]]も行基が自ら刻み、女人安産守護のために安置したという。 [[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）[[織田信長]]、[[織田信孝]]による高野攻め（[[紀州征伐]]）の時兵火にあい、地蔵堂は焼失したが、本尊は人里離れた山中に難をのがれたのを村人によって発見された。その地を"かくれがた"と名づけられている。 その後、[[紀州徳川家]]の祖・[[徳川頼宣]](南龍公)によって地蔵菩薩を安置する堂が建立され、[[正保]]5年（[[1648年]]）田園2町歩が本尊香花の費として供養され、頼宣は11回に亘って参詣した。 == 関連項目 == * [[普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)]] == 注釈・出典 == {{reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com 地蔵寺]（公式サイト） * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html 子安地蔵寺]（橋本市観光協会） {{デフォルトソート:こやすしそうし}} [[Category:和歌山県の寺]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:地蔵菩薩を本尊とする寺]] [[Category:橋本市の建築物]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=和歌山県}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

Shrines are generally divided into those enshrining [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], who is also the deity of [[Mount Fuji]] (and the main deity of [[Sengen Shrine]]), and those enshrining [[Kushinadahime]], the wife of [[Susanoo]]. revision.text:
'''Koyasu Shrine''' (子安神社) is a type of [[Shinto shrine]] primarily dedicated to deities associated with [[safe childbirth]] and [[child-rearing]].
Shrines are generally divided into those enshrining [[Konohanasakuya-hime]], who is also the deity of [[Mount Fuji]] (and the main deity of [[Sengen Shrine]]), and those enshrining [[Kushinadahime]], the wife of [[Susanoo]]. Both deities are genealogically related, with the former being the child and the latter being the grandchild of [[Ōyamatsumi]].
== Kanto Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Mito)|ja|子安神社 (水戸市)}} - Located in Chidami, Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kasumigaura)|ja|子安神社 (かすみがうら市)}} - Located in Higashinoji, Kasumigaura City, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Tone)|ja|子安神社 (利根町)}} - Located in Obusa, Tone Town, Kita-Soma District, Ibaraki Prefecture. * {{ill|Sakurai Koyasu Shrine|ja|櫻井子安神社}} - Located in Sakurai, Asahi City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Sosa)|ja|子安神社 (匝瑳市)}} - Located in Yokoshiba, Sosa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hanamigawa)|ja|子安神社 (千葉市花見川区)}} - Located in Hatamachi, Hanamigawa Ward, Chiba City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ichikawa)|ja|子安神社 (市川市)}} - Located in Kashiwaimachi, Ichikawa City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Narashino)|ja|子安神社 (習志野市)}} - Located in Fujisaki, Narashino City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Matsudo)|ja|子安神社 (松戸市)}} - Located in Yagasaki, Matsudo City, Chiba Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine|ja|子安八幡神社}} - Located in Nakaiikegami and Kitakojiya, Ota Ward, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hachioji)|ja|子安神社 (八王子市)}} - Located in Hachioji City, Tokyo. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Isehara)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢原市)}} - Located in Ishida, Isehara City, Kanagawa Prefecture.
== Chubu Region == * {{ill|Koyasumiya Shrine|ja|子安宮澤神社}} - Located in Nigioka Town, Otsuki City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachiman Shrine (Kofu)|ja|子安八幡神社 (甲府市)}} - Located in Kokubo, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kawazu)|ja|子安神社 (河津町)}} - Located in Nawa, Kawazu Town, Kamo District, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Shimbashi Sengen Shrine|ja|新橋浅間神社}} - Koyasu Shrine as a subordinate shrine of Shimbashi Sengen Shrine, Gotemba City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安神社 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Sugita, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Hachimangu (Fujinomiya)|ja|子安八幡宮 (富士宮市)}} - Located in Numakubo, Fujinomiya City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Hamamatsu)|ja|子安神社 (浜松市東区)}} - Located in Shiratori Town, Hamamatsu City, Shizuoka Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Gifu)|ja|子安神社 (岐阜市)}} - Located in Hikosaka, Gifu City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ogaki)|ja|子安神社 (大垣市)}} - Located in Akasaka Town, Ogaki City, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Obu)|ja|子安神社 (大府市)}} - Located in Kyowa Town, Obu City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kaga)|ja|子安神社 (加賀市)}} - Located in Kosugawara Town, Kaga City, Ishikawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kanazawa)|ja|子安神社 (金沢市)}} - Located in Shimoasahara Town, Kanazawa City, Ishikawa Prefecture.
== Kinki Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Ise)|ja|子安神社 (伊勢市)}} - Located in Ise City, Mie Prefecture, under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}. * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Kihoku)|ja|子安神社 (紀宝町)}} - Located in Jinai, Kiho Town, Minami-Muro District, Mie Prefecture.
== Chugoku Region == * {{ill|Koyasu Shrine (Okayama)|ja|子安神社 (岡山市)}} - Located in Kita Ward, Okayama City, Okayama Prefecture. A subordinate shrine of {{ill|Kibitsu Shrine|ja|吉備津彦神社}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Komori Shrine|ja|子守神社}} * {{ill|Kosodate Myōjin|ja|子育明神}} * {{ill|Koyasu Jizō|ja|子安地蔵}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Hall|ja|子安地蔵堂}} ** {{ill|Koyasu Jizō Temple|ja|子安地蔵寺}} * {{ill|Komori Jizō|ja|子守地蔵}} * {{ill|Kosodate Jizō|ja|子育地蔵}} * {{ill|Suitengū|ja|水天宮}}

[[伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所]]第57番 |公式HP = http://hananotera.com |公式HP名 = 子安地蔵寺 公式ウェブサイト |地図 = Japan Wakayama }} '''子安地蔵寺'''（こやすじぞうじ）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は易産山（いさんざん）。[[本尊]]は[[地蔵菩薩]]。正式名は易産山護国院地蔵寺であり、子安地蔵寺は通称である。[[フジ (植物)|藤]]の寺として知られる。 == 歴史 == 当寺は[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）の春に[[行基]]によって創建されたという。本尊の[[地蔵菩薩]]像は行基が自ら彫ったとされているもので、「日本最勝・日本最古の地蔵菩薩」であるという。この地蔵菩薩は安産祈願のためにこの地に安置されたもので、子安地蔵と呼ばれている[https://hananotera.com/history 子安地蔵寺 お寺の歴史]。 [[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に[[織田信長]]・[[織田信孝|信孝]]父子による[[高野山]]攻め（[[紀州征伐]]）の際に兵火にあって伽藍は尽く焼失したというが、本尊は人里離れた山中に難を逃れ、後に村人によって発見された。その地は「かくれがた」と呼ばれ、今も残されている。 [[江戸時代]]になると、[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]の姫君の難産を救ったことにより頼宣から篤い帰依を受け、以後[[紀州徳川家]]代々安産祈願所とされた。当寺は、[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）に高野山の[[高室院]]を本寺とし、現在の[[橋本市]]内にある11の末寺を束ねる中本寺となった。後の[[天明]]4年（[[1784年]]）には1か寺追加されている。 正保5年（[[1648年]]）に頼宣によって伽藍が再興された。また、同年には頼宣より田園2町歩が本尊香花の費として供養され、以降頼宣は11回に亘って参詣した。それ以来、歴代藩主を始めとして紀州藩士にも度々参詣されるようになった。 == 境内 == * 本堂 * 庫裏 * 水子地蔵堂 * 鐘楼 * [[フジ (植物)|藤]]の庭 * 山門（二階建て長屋門） == 前後の札所 == ;[[関西花の寺二十五霊場]] :23 [[金剛寺 (五條市)|金剛寺]]　-　'''24 子安地蔵寺'''　-　25 [[観心寺]] ;[[紀伊之国十三仏霊場]] :4 [[法輪寺 (和歌山市)|法輪寺]]　-　'''5 子安地蔵寺'''　-　6 [[慈尊院]] ;[[紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場]] :'''2 子安地蔵寺''' ;[[伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所]] :'''24 子安地蔵寺''' == 所在地 == * 和歌山県橋本市菖蒲谷94 == アクセス == * [[南海電気鉄道]][[南海高野線|高野線]] [[御幸辻駅]]より徒歩約25分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com 地蔵寺]（公式サイト） * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html 子安地蔵寺]（橋本市観光協会） {{デフォルトソート:こやすしそうし}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:和歌山県の寺]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:地蔵菩薩を本尊とする寺]] [[Category:橋本市の建築物]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=和歌山県}}

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺

This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq-Koyasu Jizō Temple-qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}

Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581年}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} [[Category:New]]

In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year }} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784年}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} [[Category:New]]

（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737年}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year }}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year }} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645年}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year }} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year }}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648年}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} [[Category:New]]

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year }}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year }}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year }}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year }} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645th year }}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year }} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year }}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year }} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year }}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} [[Category:New]]

Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year }} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year }}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year }} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year }}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9th year }} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year }}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year }} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645th year }}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year }} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year }}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year }} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year }}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year }} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year }}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9th year }} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year }}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year }} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645th year }}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year }} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year }}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year }} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year }}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}

In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}} restored the temple buildings. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645th year|12=draft}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|12=draft}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year|12=draft}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=draft}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=draft}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=draft}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=draft}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=draft}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=draft}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=draft}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=draft}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=draft}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|12=draft}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|12=draft}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}} |principal_image = {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year|12=draft}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=draft}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=draft}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=draft}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=draft}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Shingon Buddhist temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|ja|高野山真言宗|12=draft}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=draft}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=draft}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year|12=draft}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=draft}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝|12=draft}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|ja|紀州徳川家|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2nd year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1645 AD|ja|1645th year|12=draft}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=draft}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|ja|高野山|12=draft}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year|12=draft}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=draft}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=draft}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=draft}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=draft}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=draft}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=draft}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=draft}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}

{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=draft}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]

It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|737 AD|ja|737th year|12=draft}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|ja|行基|12=draft}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1581 AD|ja|1581st year|12=draft}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=draft}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|ja|織田信長|12=draft}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|ja|織田信孝|12=draft}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|ja|紀州藩|12=draft}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1784 AD|ja|1784th year|12=draft}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5th year|12=draft}} ({{ill|1648 AD|ja|1648th year|12=draft}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|ja|徳川頼宣|12=draft}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=draft}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=draft}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|ja|観心寺|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=draft}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=draft}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|ja|慈尊院|12=draft}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=draft}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=draft}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=draft}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|ja|御幸辻駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Nankai Kōya Line|ja|南海高野線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon-shū temples]] [[Category:Temples dedicated to Jizō Bosatsu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koyasu Jizō Temple}}
