27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:子安地蔵寺 up to rev 103614904
revision.text:
{{日本の寺院 |名称 = 子安地蔵寺 |画像 = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
本堂（左奥）と水子地蔵尊（手前右） |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]菖蒲谷94 |位置 = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |山号 = 易産山（易產山） |院号 = 護國院 |宗派 = [[高野山真言宗]] |本尊 = [[地蔵菩薩]] |創建年 = [[天平]]9年（[[737年]]） |開山 = [[行基]] |正式名 = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易產山護國院地藏寺） |別称 = 子安地蔵寺 |札所等 = [[関西花の寺二十五霊場]]第24番
[[紀伊之国十三仏霊場]]第5番
[[紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場]]第2番
[[伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所]]第57番 |公式HP = http://hananotera.com |公式HP名 = 子安地蔵寺 公式ウェブサイト |地図 = Japan Wakayama }} '''子安地蔵寺'''（こやすじぞうじ）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]にある[[高野山真言宗]]の[[寺院]]。[[山号]]は易産山（いさんざん）。[[本尊]]は[[地蔵菩薩]]。正式名は易産山護国院地蔵寺であり、子安地蔵寺は通称である。[[フジ (植物)|藤]]の寺として知られる。 == 歴史 == 当寺は[[天平]]9年（[[737年]]）の春に[[行基]]によって創建されたという。本尊の[[地蔵菩薩]]像は行基が自ら彫ったとされているもので、「日本最勝・日本最古の地蔵菩薩」であるという。この地蔵菩薩は安産祈願のためにこの地に安置されたもので、子安地蔵と呼ばれている[https://hananotera.com/history 子安地蔵寺 お寺の歴史]。 [[天正]]9年（[[1581年]]）に[[織田信長]]・[[織田信孝|信孝]]父子による[[高野山]]攻め（[[紀州征伐]]）の際に兵火にあって伽藍は尽く焼失したというが、本尊は人里離れた山中に難を逃れ、後に村人によって発見された。その地は「かくれがた」と呼ばれ、今も残されている。 [[江戸時代]]になると、[[紀州藩]]主[[徳川頼宣]]の姫君の難産を救ったことにより頼宣から篤い帰依を受け、以後[[紀州徳川家]]代々安産祈願所とされた。当寺は、[[正保]]2年（[[1645年]]）に高野山の[[高室院]]を本寺とし、現在の[[橋本市]]内にある11の末寺を束ねる中本寺となった。後の[[天明]]4年（[[1784年]]）には1か寺追加されている。 正保5年（[[1648年]]）に頼宣によって伽藍が再興された。また、同年には頼宣より田園2町歩が本尊香花の費として供養され、以降頼宣は11回に亘って参詣した。それ以来、歴代藩主を始めとして紀州藩士にも度々参詣されるようになった。 == 境内 == * 本堂 * 庫裏 * 水子地蔵堂 * 鐘楼 * [[フジ (植物)|藤]]の庭 * 山門（二階建て長屋門） == 前後の札所 == ;[[関西花の寺二十五霊場]] :23 [[金剛寺 (五條市)|金剛寺]]　-　'''24 子安地蔵寺'''　-　25 [[観心寺]] ;[[紀伊之国十三仏霊場]] :4 [[法輪寺 (和歌山市)|法輪寺]]　-　'''5 子安地蔵寺'''　-　6 [[慈尊院]] ;[[紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場]] :'''2 子安地蔵寺''' ;[[伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所]] :'''24 子安地蔵寺''' == 所在地 == * 和歌山県橋本市菖蒲谷94 == アクセス == * [[南海電気鉄道]][[南海高野線|高野線]] [[御幸辻駅]]より徒歩約25分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)]] == 外部リンク == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com 地蔵寺]（公式サイト） * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html 子安地蔵寺]（橋本市観光協会） {{デフォルトソート:こやすしそうし}} {{Buddhism-stub}} [[Category:和歌山県の寺]] [[Category:通称]] [[Category:高野山真言宗の寺院]] [[Category:地蔵菩薩を本尊とする寺]] [[Category:橋本市の建築物]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=和歌山県}}

revision.text:
{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|qq=}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 [[:en:Jison-in|Jison-in]] ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from [[:en:Miyukitsuji Station|Miyukitsuji Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{translated page|ja|子安地蔵寺|version=103614904|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Common names]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]]

During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 revision.text:
{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|qq=}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 [[:en:Jison-in|Jison-in]] ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from [[:en:Miyukitsuji Station|Miyukitsuji Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{translated page|ja|子安地蔵寺|version=103614904|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|qq=}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 [[:en:Jison-in|Jison-in]] ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from [[:en:Miyukitsuji Station|Miyukitsuji Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{translated page|ja|子安地蔵寺|version=103614904|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 revision.text:
{{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 [[:en:Jison-in|Jison-in]] ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from [[:en:Miyukitsuji Station|Miyukitsuji Station]] on the [[:en:Koya Line|Nankai Kōya Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{translated page|ja|子安地蔵寺|version=103614904|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Koyasu Jizō Temple]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|qid=Q842098}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.337528|N|135.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Koyasu-gami|qid=Q132705423}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447886]] Wikidata name: Koyasu jizō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|qid=Q842098}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.337528|N|135.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Koyasu-gami|qid=Q132705423}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11447886]] Wikidata name: Koyasu jizō-ji [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|qq=|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|qid=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|qid=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|qid=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25
