（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|qid=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|qq=|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|qid=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|qq=|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|qq=|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|qq=|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506290}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506290}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] |principal_image = [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) |founder = [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a [[:en:Buddhist temple|Shingon Buddhist temple]] of the [[:en:Kōyasan Shingon-shū|Kōyasan Shingon-shū]] sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]]. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ([[:en:AD 737|737 AD]]) by [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]]. The temple’s principal image, a statue of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]], is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ([[:en:1581|1581 AD]]), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by [[:en:Oda Nobunaga|Oda Nobunaga]] and his son [[:en:Oda Nobutaka|Oda Nobutaka]], the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the temple gained significant patronage from [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]], lord of the [[:en:Kishū Domain|Kishū Domain]], after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the [[:en:Kishū Tokugawa family|Kishū Tokugawa family]]. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ([[:en:1645|1645 AD]]), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on [[:en:Mount Kōya|Mount Kōya]], overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ([[:en:1784|1784 AD]]). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ([[:en:1648|1648 AD]]), [[:en:Tokugawa Yorinobu|Tokugawa Yorinobu]] restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[:en:Kanshin-ji|Kanshin-ji]] ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506290}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 （易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Shingon Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ({{ill|AD 737|en|AD 737|WD=Q23583|lt=737 AD}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ({{ill|1581|en|1581|WD=Q6717|lt=1581 AD}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|en|Oda Nobutaka|WD=Q2614349|lt=Oda Nobutaka}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishū Tokugawa family}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ({{ill|1645|en|1645|WD=Q6925|lt=1645 AD}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|WD=Q7752|lt=1784 AD}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ({{ill|1648|en|1648|WD=Q6941|lt=1648 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|en|Kanshin-ji|WD=Q1088470|lt=Kanshin-ji}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506290}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|en|Jison-in|WD=Q3179279|lt=Jison-in}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Station|WD=Q6884576|lt=Miyukitsuji Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506292}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|WD=Q7752|lt=1784 AD}}). [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.337528|N|135.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Koyasu-gami|WD=Q132705423}}}} {{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} |principal_image = {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ({{ill|AD 737|en|AD 737|WD=Q23583|lt=737 AD}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Shingon Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ({{ill|AD 737|en|AD 737|WD=Q23583|lt=737 AD}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ({{ill|1581|en|1581|WD=Q6717|lt=1581 AD}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|en|Oda Nobutaka|WD=Q2614349|lt=Oda Nobutaka}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishū Tokugawa family}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ({{ill|1645|en|1645|WD=Q6925|lt=1645 AD}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|WD=Q7752|lt=1784 AD}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ({{ill|1648|en|1648|WD=Q6941|lt=1648 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Hashimoto|WD=Q842098}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.337528|N|135.582389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Koyasu-gami|WD=Q132705423}}}} {{Japanese Temple |name = {{ill|Koyasu Jizō-ji|ja|子安地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Jizō-ji|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |image = [[File:KoyasuJizouji005.JPG|250px]]
Main Hall (left back) and Mizuko Jizō-son (front right) |location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 |coordinates = {{Coord|34|20|15.1|N|135|34|56.6|E}} |mountain_name = {{ill|Isan-zan|ja|易産山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isan-zan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 子安地蔵寺|1=Koyasu jizō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447886}} |temple_name = {{ill|Gokoku-in|ja|護國院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku-in|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 護国院|1=Gokoku-in Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632505}} |sect = {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} |principal_image = {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} |founded = {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ({{ill|AD 737|en|AD 737|WD=Q23583|lt=737 AD}}) |founder = {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}} |formal_name = 易産山 護国院 地蔵寺
（易産山護國院地藏寺） |common_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji |pilgrimage = * {{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} No. 24 * {{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} No. 5 * {{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} No. 2 * {{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} No. 57 |official_website = http://hananotera.com |official_name = Koyasu Jizō-ji Official Website |map = Japan Wakayama }} '''Koyasu Jizō-ji''' (子安地蔵寺) is a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=Shingon Buddhist temple}} of the {{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|en|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|WD=Q3077357|lt=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}} sect, located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its {{ill|Mountain name (sangō)|ja|山号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain name (sangō)|1=sangō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11058522}} is *Isan-zan* (易産山), and its principal image is {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}. The temple’s formal name is *Isan-zan Gokoku-in Jizō-ji* (易産山護国院地蔵寺), while *Koyasu Jizō-ji* is a commonly used name. It is well known for its wisteria flowers. == History == The temple was founded in the spring of {{ill|Tenpyō 9|ja|天平9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506276}} ({{ill|AD 737|en|AD 737|WD=Q23583|lt=737 AD}}) by {{ill|Gyōki|en|Gyōki|WD=Q1136664|lt=Gyōki}}. The temple’s principal image, a statue of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}}, is said to have been carved by Gyōki himself and is known as the "Most Victorious and Oldest Jizō Bosatsu in Japan." This Jizō was enshrined at the temple to provide prayers for safe childbirth, leading to its name *Koyasu Jizō* (子安地蔵).[https://hananotera.com/history History of Koyasu Jizō-ji] In {{ill|Tenshō 9|ja|天正9年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō 9|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506281}} ({{ill|1581|en|1581|WD=Q6717|lt=1581 AD}}), during the {{ill|Kōyasan Campaign|ja|高野山攻め|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōyasan Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506282}} led by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|WD=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga}} and his son {{ill|Oda Nobutaka|en|Oda Nobutaka|WD=Q2614349|lt=Oda Nobutaka}}, the temple was set ablaze, and all its structures were destroyed. However, the principal image was hidden deep in the mountains and later discovered by villagers. The location where it was hidden is still known today as *Kakuregata* (かくれがた). During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the temple gained significant patronage from {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}}, lord of the {{ill|Kishū Domain|en|Kishū Domain|WD=Q1069187|lt=Kishū Domain}}, after it was said to have helped his daughter through a difficult childbirth. Koyasu Jizō-ji subsequently became the official prayer temple for safe childbirth for the {{ill|Kishū Tokugawa family|en|Kishū Tokugawa family|WD=Q11605046|lt=Kishū Tokugawa family}}. In {{ill|Shōhō 2|ja|正保2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506284}} ({{ill|1645|en|1645|WD=Q6925|lt=1645 AD}}), the temple was designated as a branch temple under {{ill|Takashitsu-in|ja|高室院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashitsu-in|WD=Q135506286}} on {{ill|Mount Kōya|en|Mount Kōya|WD=Q535065|lt=Mount Kōya}}, overseeing 11 sub-temples in the present-day Hashimoto area. Another temple was added to its jurisdiction in {{ill|Tenmei 4|ja|天明4年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmei 4|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506287}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|WD=Q7752|lt=1784 AD}}). In {{ill|Shōhō 5|ja|正保5年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōhō 5|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506289}} ({{ill|1648|en|1648|WD=Q6941|lt=1648 AD}}), {{ill|Tokugawa Yorinobu|en|Tokugawa Yorinobu|WD=Q1045763|lt=Tokugawa Yorinobu}} restored the temple buildings. He also granted the temple 2 *chōbu* (町歩) of farmland to cover the costs of worship and offerings for the principal image. Yorinobu visited the temple 11 times, and from then on, successive lords of the Kishū Domain and their retainers made frequent pilgrimages. == Temple Grounds == * Main Hall (本堂) * Kuri (庫裏) - Temple living quarters * Mizuko Jizō Hall (水子地蔵堂) * Bell Tower (鐘楼) * Wisteria Garden (藤の庭) * Mountain Gate (山門) - Two-story Nagaya-mon (長屋門) == Pilgrimage Routes == ;{{ill|Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|ja|関西花の寺二十五霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansai Hana no Tera 25 Reijō|1=Kansai Flower Temple 25 Sacred Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11656383}} :23 {{ill|Kongō-ji (Gojō)|ja|金剛寺 (五條市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō-ji (Gojō)|1=Kongō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223150}} → **24 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 25 {{ill|Kanshin-ji|en|Kanshin-ji|WD=Q1088470|lt=Kanshin-ji}} ;{{ill|Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊之国十三仏霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|1=Kii Province Thirteen Buddhas Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q135236620}} :4 {{ill|Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja|法輪寺 (和歌山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrin-ji (Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506290}} → **5 Koyasu Jizō-ji** → 6 {{ill|Jison-in|en|Jison-in|WD=Q3179279|lt=Jison-in}} ;{{ill|Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja|紀伊西国三十三所観音霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Saigoku 33 Kannon Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506277}} :**2 Koyasu Jizō-ji** ;{{ill|Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja|伊都郡四国八十八ヵ所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itogun Shikoku 88 Temples Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506279}} :**57 Koyasu Jizō-ji** == Location == * {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja|和歌山県橋本市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashimoto, Wakayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Shōbudani 94 == Access == * Approx. 25-minute walk from {{ill|Miyukitsuji Station|en|Miyukitsuji Station|WD=Q6884576|lt=Miyukitsuji Station}} on the {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|WD=Q71986|lt=Nankai Kōya Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja|普賢寺 (橋本市菖蒲谷)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fugen-ji (Hashimoto, Wakayama)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506292}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:Koyasu Jizouji}} * [http://hananotera.com Jizō-ji] (Official Website) * [http://www.hashimoto-kanko.net/kanko/pg86.html Koyasu Jizō-ji] (Hashimoto City Tourism Association) {{translated page|ja|子安地蔵寺|version=103614904|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:子安地蔵寺]] {{wikidata link|Q11447886}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Hashimoto,
